title: 'Sorry papa': Kanpur BTech gold medallist dies by suicide after 50 failed job attempts
content: Tragedy struck in Kanpur as a 23-year-old BTech gold medallist ended his life, having faced the daunting challenge of applying for a government position more than fifty times without success. His poignant suicide note expressed sorrow towards his father for not meeting expectations. The police have launched an investigation and are exploring the likelihood that depression contributed to this sorrowful decision.

title: ‘Security hole’ enabled US-Israel bombing of Ayatollah’s residence: Iran minister Araghchi
content: Iranian foreign minister Seyyed Abbas Araghchi alleged a security breach allowed foreign powers access.  This breach reportedly provided intelligence and influenced decision-making during a conflict.  He stated attackers knew Ayatollah Ali Khamenei's routine and targeted his residence precisely.  A regional leader had warned Iran about leadership being targeted before the conflict began.  Araghchi noted the foreign ministry remained operational throughout the intense 40-day war.

title: 'No permission sought or granted': Delhi Police on CJP's Parliament march
content: Delhi Police has denied permission for a proposed march to Parliament on July 20. Prohibitory orders are in effect across the New Delhi district, banning assemblies. Unauthorized gatherings will face legal action under new Bharatiya Nyaya Sanhita provisions. The Delhi High Court previously upheld the Centre's intervention in Sonam Wangchuk's hunger strike. Authorities urge public cooperation to ensure security during the upcoming Parliament session.

title: WC final preview: Why Spain vs Argentina is much more than Messi vs Yamal
content: Spain and Argentina meet in a World Cup final loaded with footballing destiny, La Masia symbolism, Messi’s last miracle, Yamal’s first coronation and enough off-field politics to make FIFA’s “football unites the world” slogan file for leave. Read more in today's OFFSIDE.

title: Airbnb CEO says will keep investing in New York despite Mamdani's rent freeze plan
content: Airbnb and Anthropic are expanding their New York City operations significantly.  This investment occurs despite concerns from business leaders about city policies.  Airbnb purchased a building for its growing New York workforce.  Anthropic will lease a large office building and double its staff.  These expansions highlight continued business activity amidst policy debates.

title: Gurnoor Brar enters unwanted record books with nightmare spell at Lord's
content: Gurnoor Brar recorded the most expensive ODI bowling figures at Lord's. His spell of 0 for 97 entered India's record books for expensive spells. England's top order dominated an inexperienced Indian bowling attack. Ben Duckett and Jacob Bethell built a strong opening partnership. England's total of 387 for 3 became the highest ODI score at Lord's.

title: 'Months of torture': Bengaluru woman dies by suicide after 'dowry harassment'
content: A 31-year-old woman allegedly died by suicide after enduring dowry harassment. Her husband and father-in-law are accused of causing her distress. The deceased's father filed a complaint with the police regarding the harassment. Police arrested the husband and father-in-law following the complaint. Both accused individuals have been remanded to judicial custody.

title: US judge to Meta's laid off employees: Cannot stop the company from job cuts
content: A US judge has allowed Meta Platforms to proceed with planned layoffs of 26 employees. These employees had sued the company alleging AI-driven discrimination in job cuts. The judge declined an emergency request to halt the layoffs while claims proceed. He stated the court may reconsider if new evidence emerges about AI usage. Meta denies wrongdoing and states human decisions guided the workforce reduction.

title: Joe Root scripts history, becomes first England batter to...
content: Joe Root achieved a historic milestone with six consecutive fifty-plus scores.  He scored an unbeaten 74 off 49 balls at Lord's.  England posted a strong 387 for 3 after batting first.  Ben Duckett and Jacob Bethell provided a solid opening partnership.  Jos Buttler finished with a quick 41 not out.

title: Real-life 'gilmore girls' pilot house listed for $2.7 million in Ontario
content: A historic Unionville home, which served as Lorelai and Rory Gilmore’s house in the pilot episode, is now for sale. This property, listed at $3.88 million CAD, offers a tangible connection to the beloved show. The surrounding neighborhood also featured as key Stars Hollow locations, enhancing its appeal. The house, built around 1875, blends Victorian charm with modern amenities for comfortable living.

title: Scientists discover a ‘third cell’ that helps regrow hair
content: In a groundbreaking development, Japanese researchers have unveiled a pivotal third cell integral to the regeneration of hair follicles. This finding holds the potential to reverse conditions like baldness and thinning hair by collaborating with two established cell types. Successful experiments conducted on mice demonstrated the complete cycle of hair growth, paving the way for pioneering regenerative treatments aimed at combating hair loss.

title: 'He intends to continue the fast': Sonam Wangchuk's wife vows to lead Parliament march
content: Gitanjali J Angmo will lead a march to Parliament representing her husband, Sonam Wangchuk. Wangchuk continues his indefinite hunger strike from Safdarjung Hospital after being moved there. The Delhi High Court declined to order his immediate transfer to a private facility. Angmo urged protesters to maintain peace and remain vigilant against disruptions. Security has been heightened across New Delhi ahead of the planned march.

title: PMAY-Urban: UP CM hands over first house keys in Ghaziabad - here's all you need to know
content: Chief minister Yogi Adityanath handed over the first PMAY-U keys in Ghaziabad.  The scheme aims to provide affordable housing to economically disadvantaged sections of society.  Funds disbursal and infrastructure work had previously delayed the project's rollout.  The Chief minister also inaugurated and laid foundation stones for 90 other development projects.  These projects include a flyover and a multi-level parking facility for the city.

title: 'Carry Tiranga, no political flags': CJP releases do's & don'ts for 'Chalo Sansad' march
content: The Cockroach Janta Party released guidelines for the 'Chalo Sansad' march. Participants are urged to maintain peace and carry national symbols. Activist Sonam Wangchuk called the march India's second freedom movement. Delhi Police have deployed additional personnel and intensified security measures. Authorities are determined to prevent any unauthorized march towards Parliament.

title: Streamer University 2026 ending date explained as Kai Cenat reveals what happens before July 20
content: The grand finale of Streamer University 2026, hosted by Kai Cenat, is set for July 20th morning. Alumni have begun to flock to campus to engage with students in these last days of festivities. Kai has made it clear that any rule infractions could lead to expulsion from campus. This year's event boasts a longer duration than its predecessor, designed to maximize creative collaboration and ensure a vibrant atmosphere.

title: How much prize money does the FIFA World Cup 2026 third-place winner get? Full payout explained
content: The 2026 FIFA World Cup will award twenty-nine million dollars for third place. The fourth-place team will receive twenty-seven million dollars after the playoff. This prize money is separate from preparation payments given to nations. The champions will earn fifty million dollars and runners-up thirty-three million dollars. FIFA confirmed prize money distribution for all tournament stages.

title: Former NHL star Mike Commodore warns Oilers parents at development camp
content: Ex-NHL defenseman Mike Commodore has escalated his years-long war on the Edmonton Oilers by issuing a warning to parents attending the club’s development camp. The social media post went viral across the NHL, reigniting discussion of Commodore’s past with former coach Mike Babcock, and drawing mixed reactions from hockey fans and analysts.

title: Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce frustrated over wedding controversy: Report
content: The Kansas City Chiefs popular tight end Travis Kelce is all set to bounce  back on the field for his 15th season in the league, weeks after marrying Taylor Swift, as millions cheer him on. While both Travis Kelce and Taylor Swift have remained strictly private about their lives, it seems the two are now anticipating a few issues in their lives.

title: Ashwin questions Kuldeep's omission, says repeated snubs 'can affect confidence'
content: Ravichandran Ashwin questioned India's decision to omit Kuldeep Yadav.  The spinner was again left out of the side for the third ODI.  This repeated exclusion could negatively impact his confidence and rhythm.  India opted for a pace-heavy attack despite bowling absences.  Kuldeep has now missed all ODIs and T20Is on the tour.

title: Maharashtra allows restaurants, hotels to stay open till 4am for FIFA World Cup final
content: Maharashtra eateries can now stay open until 4 am on July 20. This allows football fans to watch the Argentina-Spain World Cup final publicly.  Hospitality bodies had requested this extension for licensed establishments.  Other cities like Delhi and Bengaluru have also announced similar relaxations.  The final match will begin early Sunday morning, Indian time.

title: 8 killed, 12 injured as multiple landslides hit Nagaland's Mon district
content: Eight people died and twelve were injured after landslides struck Mon district in Nagaland. Heavy rainfall triggered the massive landslides across multiple locations on Sunday morning. Search and rescue operations are underway involving SDRF, police, and NDRF teams. Several houses were affected, and road clearance work is being carried out urgently. Authorities advise residents in vulnerable areas to remain alert due to continued rainfall forecasts.

title: Tanishk Bagchi calls Rs 8 lakh 'Saiyaara' royalty 'peanuts'; YRF says dues paid as per contract
content: Music composer Tanishk Bagchi voiced dissatisfaction with royalty earnings for the 'Saiyaara' title track. He stated the financial returns did not match his dedicated creative efforts. Yash Raj Films responded, clarifying the song was a collaboration with three composers. Royalties are being shared equally among Tanishk Bagchi, Faheem Abdullah, and Arslan Nizami. All payments were made according to contractual terms and agreed-upon arrangements.

title: Decomposed body of med student found inside Bengaluru house; foul smell alerts family
content: A 20-year-old paramedical student was found dead in his Bengaluru home.  His parents discovered his decomposed body hanging in his room on Friday.  The student, Udhay Gowda, had last been seen four days prior.  Police suspect suicide but are investigating the exact cause of death.  His mobile phone was seized for further forensic examination.

title: 'Avoid cloak-and-dagger tactics': Derek O'Brien writes to PM Modi, seeks all-party meet
content: Trinamool Congress leader Derek O'Brien urged Prime Minister Modi to convene an all-party meeting. He expressed concerns over proposed delimitation legislation and the Foreign Contribution Amendment bill. O'Brien described these measures as 'draconian' and 'cowboy legislation' in his letter. The opposition fears the delimitation exercise could reduce southern states' parliamentary representation. The Foreign Contribution Amendment bill may tighten government oversight of charitable organizations.

title: 'War can’t be solution': Visit to Nagasaki museum leaves Indian footballers speechless
content: Young Indian footballers on the Reliance Football Development League's exposure trip to Japan visited the Nagasaki Atomic Bomb Museum, gaining a powerful lesson on the horrors of war and resilience. Coaches Israil Gurung and Praveen Kumar said the visit highlighted the importance of peace and showed how Japan rebuilt after devastation, inspiring players to overcome setbacks with determination.

title: 'Govt was within its right': Why Delhi HC backed Sonam Wangchuk's hospital shift
content: The Delhi high court rejected a plea to move Sonam Wangchuk to a private hospital.  The court noted no force was used in his transfer to Safdarjung Hospital.  Family members have been granted round-the-clock access to the fasting activist.  Doctors are monitoring his health and administering oral substances with consent.  The government acted within its rights to provide care for Wangchuk.

title: ‘No state has witnessed such a political change since Independence’: Amit Shah on Bengal
content: Union Home Minister Amit Shah called West Bengal's political change unprecedented since Independence. He criticized the previous Left Front and Trinamool Congress governments. Shah attended the foundation stone laying for a new dairy project. This project aims to boost the state's dairy sector and farmer incomes. The minister concluded his three-day visit to West Bengal.

title: Salman Khan's latest appearance at a Mumbai event leaves fans worried
content: Salman Khan's latest appearance has ignited discussions among fans concerning his health status. Many fans couldn't help but reminisce about his significant battles with trigeminal neuralgia—a condition that causes excruciating facial pain. He has opened up about the severe pain and difficulties he faced while eating. Fortunately, a gamma knife surgery brought him considerable relief. Nonetheless, he has remained silent about his current health condition.

title: Maple Leafs' Gavin McKenna trains with Sharks star Macklin Celebrini
content: Toronto Maple Leafs’ prospect Gavin McKenna was recently spotted in a competitive offseason skate in Vancouver with some of hockey's brightest young talent. The training session gave fans a glimpse of the next generation of the NHL with San Jose Sharks forward Macklin Celebrini, Chicago Blackhawks prospect Michael Misa and San Jose Sharks defenseman Sam Dickinson.

title: 'Spain will win easily': Ronaldo makes bold Argentina prediction before WC final
content: Brazilian legend Ronaldo Nazario predicts Spain will easily defeat Argentina in the World Cup final. He believes Spain's possession-based game will prove too much for Lionel Messi's team. Unbeaten Spain enters the championship match with a strong defensive record. Argentina seeks its fourth world title and aims to defend its crown. Ronaldo's prediction stems from Spain and France being his tournament favorites.

title: Why China may not supply J-35 stealth fighters to Pakistan anytime soon
content: China's planned J-35 fighter jet export to Pakistan is hitting roadblocks. Analysts believe that Beijing is more inclined to concentrate on its military upgrades rather than facilitating this deal. Providing sophisticated technology could lead to an unstable military environment in South Asia. Moreover, China's desire to protect its sensitive military technologies adds another layer of complexity, making the early delivery of the J-35 to Pakistan quite unlikely.

title: 'NCPI recognised by EC': Sudip Bandyopadhyay hits back after oppn's walkout
content: The Nationalist Citizens Party of India attended an all-party meeting ahead of the Monsoon Session. Opposition parties staged a symbolic walkout to protest the invitation extended to NCPI leaders. NCPI leaders defended their party's recognition by the Election Commission and its MPs' presence. They stated that the Lok Sabha Speaker would clarify seating arrangements and disqualification matters. The government defended inviting the NCPI as they had requested recognition.

title: Weekly current affairs: India's hydrogen train, Skyroot's space milestone and more
content: India enhanced naval power with a new helicopter and launched its first hydrogen train. Skyroot Aerospace achieved a historic private orbital rocket launch, while an Indian-origin astronaut began an ISS mission. Global oil markets remained stable despite Strait of Hormuz concerns. Ukraine saw government reshuffles and protests amid ongoing unrest. China reported a chipmaking breakthrough, and Japan maintained its male-only imperial succession.

title: Dombivli hospital row: Shiv Sena corporator Mhatre surrenders after HC cancels bail
content: Shiv Sena corporator Ramesh Mhatre surrendered to police after his bail was cancelled by the High Court. He will now be produced before a Kalyan court and likely sent to judicial custody. Police also arrested Sadhana Karande, the fifth accused in the hospital assault case. Karande is the mother of the pregnant woman whose treatment delay sparked the incident.

title: 'Big mistake': Abhijeet Dipke accuses police of 'kidnapping' Wangchuk
content: Climate activist Sonam Wangchuk was removed from the Jantar Mantar protest site.  He had been on an indefinite hunger strike for over twenty days.  Authorities moved him to Safdarjung Hospital for necessary medical monitoring.  Protesters and the Cockroach Janta Party criticized this forceful removal.  However, the Delhi High Court later upheld the government's decision.

title: Relief for Abhishek Banerjee: HC halts demolition drive of TMC MP's Amtala office
content: The Calcutta High Court has ordered a halt to demolition work at Abhishek Banerjee's office. This directive came after an anti-encroachment drive was initiated at the premises. The Trinamool Congress leader had alleged political motivation behind the demolition efforts. He stated the office was built legally on purchased land with permissions. The district administration claimed the structure violated building plan rules.

title: Kerala declares Monday holiday for schools and colleges after FIFA World Cup 2026 final
content: Kerala has declared a holiday for all educational institutions on Monday ahead of the FIFA World Cup 2026 final between Argentina and Spain. The decision was taken considering the match's late-night schedule, which could make it difficult for students to attend morning classes. However, pre-scheduled examinations will continue as planned. The announcement came after demands from opposition leaders and others seeking relief for students. The move highlights Kerala's football enthusiasm and student convenience.

title: No relief for Wangchuk: HC backs govt's move to shift activist to hospital, says no force used
content: The Delhi HC upheld the Centre's decision to move Sonam Wangchuk to Safdarjung Hospital.  The court found the government's action was not arbitrary given his health condition. His wife's petition for transfer to a private hospital was declined by the court.  The court noted Wangchuk's family has been given unrestricted access to him.

title: Congress blames Centre for recurring exam paper leaks
content: APCC working president says repeated examination irregularities have eroded confidence in the testing system and affected millions of students across the country

title: Supreme Court holds abuse is uncivil, but not obscene
content: In a judgment in a land dispute case in which the four-letter expletive was used, the top court says that to qualify as ‘obscenity’, the terms used must be lascivious, should appeal to prurient interests of people, and provoke a tendency to deprave and corrupt people’s minds

title: Trainee IPS accused of sexual harassment and assault by fellow officer, booked
content: According to the complaint, the alleged harassment began in late June and continued for several weeks. The woman alleged that Reddy sent her sexually abusive messages and made derogatory remarks about her in front of fellow trainees.

title: Officials told to work in mission mode to tackle potential impact of El Nino
content: Agriculture Minister Tummala Nageswara Rao chairs review meet on contingency plan for erstwhile Karimnagar district; stresses timely implementation of contingency measures, promotion of drought-tolerant and other suitable alternative crops, water conservation and farmer awareness programmes to safeguard livelihoods

title: IUML walks a tightrope over Waqf row, tells Ministers to back UDF with caution
content: Party faces pressure from various quarters over decisions perceived as a shift from its earlier positions on issues such as Waqf, PM SHRI and the government’s move to reduce taxes on low-alcohol beverages

title: Students must unite against drugs: Minister Bindu Krishna
content: She was presiding over the distribution ceremony of the ‘Focus Kollam’ Minister’s Excellence Award-2026, organised for talented students from the Kollam constituency

title: CBI arrests man for posing as government official, taking bribes
content: The accused, who allegedly impersonated an official of the Department of Animal Husbandry and Dairying, sought a bribe of ₹45,000 from the complainant, equivalent to 2% of the loan the latter had applied for

title: Two new private medical colleges, 1,300 additional medical seats for Karnataka
content: With this, Karnataka has emerged as the state with the highest number of medical seats in the country.

title: Jammu and Kashmir rains LIVE: At least 12 dead, several missing in flash floods; rescue ops. under way
content: Home Minister Amit Shah assured the L-G and the CM of all possible assistance from the Centre for the safety and security of the affected people, officials said

title: Meritorious students to be presented with ‘Shining Star’ awards in Andhra Pradesh on July 20
content: A total of 4,344 students selected for the awards will each receive a medal, a certificate of appreciation, and a cash award of ₹20,000

title: Meaningful socio-political cinema giving way to crime and violence, says Rafeeq Ahamed
content: Poet-lyricist says cinema has gradually been reduced to a form of entertainment with little lasting emotional or intellectual impact on audiences; he inaugurates celebrations marking 100th screening of Kolazhy Cinema Kottaka in Thrissur

title: Karnataka Cabinet approves ₹12-crore AI Centre of Excellence at IIIT Raichur
content: The Karnataka Cabinet has approved the establishment of a Centre of Excellence in Artificial Intelligence at the Indian Institute of Information Technology, Raichur, with an outlay of ₹12 crore over five years, Minister for Home, Information Technology & Biotechnology, and e-Governance Priyank Kharge

title: KDF slams political parties for setting age limits for leaders
content: State president P. Ramabhadran also criticised the tendency of political parties to distance themselves from talented youngsters

title: YSRCP leaders decry ‘neglect’ of Handri-Neeva canal system
content: They accuse the coalition government of systematically weakening one of Rayalaseema’s critical irrigation projects through poor execution and policy reversals, and neglecting the region’s water security

title: Congress leader Jairam Ramesh calls talk of 50% seat increase for all States an ‘optical illusion’
content: The Constitution still mandates seat reallocation in favour of more populous States unless the 1971 freeze is explicitly retained, the Congress leader says

title: Tirupati Govindadhamam vows to support needy in cremation services
content: The electric crematorium facility was jointly launched eight years back by Tirupati Municipal Corporation, with the active support of the Rotary Club of Tirupati, Arya Vysya Association and Veerabahu Society

title: YSRCP accuses government of neglecting agriculture amid rainfall deficit
content: The party leader alleges poor water management, delays in procurement and inadequate support to farmers affected during the ongoing kharif season

title: ITDA Chintoor recommends ₹4 lakh ex gratia for five tribals drowned in Godavari
content: The tribals were told to hold Gram Sabhas, preventing fishing in the river during the monsoon to prevent untoward incidents.

title: At least eight dead, 15 hurt in Nagaland landslide
content: Heavy incessant rainfall and a cloudburst caused widespread destruction at various locations in and around Mon town; State announces ex-gratia of ₹4 lakh for kin of deceased, ₹74,000 for each of the injured

title: Jagan alleges Ramayapatnam port disinvestment will hurt State finances
content: Former Chief Minister claims the proposed transaction undervalues a strategic public asset, leaves the State with over ₹3,500 crore in debt-servicing obligations and jeopardises the port’s long-term economic potential

title: Geo-tagging of forest locations raises concerns over trespassing
content: Forest department flags 23 Google Maps listings inside reserve forests in Kozhikode as misleading; officials say unauthorised map pins and uploaded photographs are encouraging illegal entry into protected areas; DFO seeks removal of the listings, warning of trespassing, public safety risks and human-wildlife conflict

title: Assam Governor highlights Sanskrit’s role in connecting  heritage and modernity
content: Inaugurating a lecture series at the NSU, Lakshman Prasad Acharya urges students to study the scientific aspects hidden in Sanskrit, decipher them, and pass on the benefits to the future generations

title: TDP calls YSRCP’s allegations on Handri-Neeva works baseless
content: The party that halted the expansion works and failed to move a basket of earth during its five-year term has no moral right to speak about it now, says Payyavula Keshav

title: Doctor assault case: Shiv Sena corporator surrenders before police, sent to judicial custody till Aug. 3
content: Bombay HC cancelled the bail granted to Ramesh Mhatre by the Kalyan Judicial Magistrate First Class Court; matter has been posted for further hearing on July 22

title: New study hints at a wider distribution of Agasthyamalai Bushfrog in Western Ghats
content: A research team has discovered the Agasthyamalai Bushfrog in the high-elevation shola forests of Devikulam, near Munnar, extending the species’ known distribution by more than 120 km northwards. Until now, the species was thought to be confined to the Agasthyamalai Hills south of the Shencottah Gap

title: Delimitation: how to align what is said and what is written
content: The Bills moved by the government did not align with the oral promise made by Prime Minister Narendra Modi and Home Minister Amit Shah on the floor of Parliament and in interactions with representatives of political parties

title: Man arrested for manufacturing drugs in school, sets up Alprazolam factory again, four held
content: An accused, Malela Jaya Prakash Goud, was arrested in September 2025 in connection with a case involving an Alprazolam manufacturing unit at Medha High School in Bowenpally and remained in judicial custody until March 16 this year

title: MP distributes tricycles to 42 Divyang in Hubballi
content: He says PM Modi has launched Rashtriya Vayoshri Yojana to see that even the most vulnerable members of society get benefits

title: Six dead, four others missing as rain and cloudbursts trigger flash floods in J&K
content: Heavy rain since July 19 morning triggers flash floods in Poonch, Rajouri and Kishtwar districts, damaging public property and washing away scores of vehicles. Multiple cloudbursts are reported from the districts as landslides disrupt traffic in these areas.

title: Police enhance night vigil across Sri Sathya Sai district to boost security
content: During the inspections, the police officials question suspicious individuals moving during midnight hours without valid reasons, collect details and fingerprints of some individuals for verification

title: Curbing talent exodus: It’s deja vu for Indian Space Research Organisation
content: In the early 1990s, the information technology boom and campus recruitments dealt a blow to ISRO which was then on an expansion mode

title: Prioritise State’s interests in Parliament, CPI(M) urges TDP and YSRCP MPs
content: Highlight the crisis faced by tobacco, maize, mango and aquaculture farmers, and seek solutions from the Union government, it suggests

title: Man found murdered in Kozhikode
content: Involvement of drug addicts suspected based on the statements of some of the local residents in the area

title: DUK partners with HEX20 to launch hands-on educational programme in satellite technology
content: The initiative aims at providing selected students from India and abroad with immersive training in the design, assembly, integration and testing of satellite subsystems

title: Centre To Table Bill To Make "Insult" To 'Vande Mataram' Criminal Offence
content: The Prevention of Insults to National Honour (Amendment) Bill, 2026, has been listed in the agenda of the Rajya Sabha for Monday, with Home Minister Amit Shah set to introduce the bill.

title: Man Stabs Wife To Death With Scissors In Delhi, Arrested From Moradabad
content: A complaint of murder was lodged with the Seelmapur police station on Thursday as the victim's family claimed that the woman was killed in a cold-blooded manner by her husband after a bitter fight.

title: FIFA World Cup Final: Kerala Schools, Colleges To Remain Closed Tomorrow
content: The decision was taken following directions from Chief Minister VD Satheesan after thousands of football enthusiasts, particularly students, appealed through social media for a holiday.

title: 16 Killed In Rain-Related Incidents Amid Monsoon Fury In North, East India
content: Jammu and Kashmir was the worst hit, with at least 11 people killed and several others missing after torrential rains triggered landslides and flash floods in the twin border districts of Poonch and Rajouri, prompting a multi-agency rescue operation.

title: Days After Indonesia Boat Sinking 2 Survivors Found On Uninhabited Island
content: The vessel, which was carrying more than 70 people, sank on Wednesday as it sailed near Selayar, a small island south of the larger Sulawesi.

title: Wife, Son Not Home, Man Sexually Assaults Teen Daughter In Delhi
content: The survivor alleged that on July 7, her father came to their residence when her mother and brother were not at home and established forcible physical relations with her against her will.

title: 3 Arrested In Ayodhya For Eating Meat, Drinking Alcohol On River Boat
content: The arrested men, Lavkush Nishad, Jitendra Nishad, and Rajkumar Nishad, were accused by the police of undermining the faith of the devotees who consider the river holy.

title: 1 Killed, 16 Injured in One of Russia's Largest Missile Barrages Yet On Kyiv
content: US President Donald Trump has said he is prepared to grant Ukraine licenses to produce Patriots, the most effective means of intercepting Russian ballistic missiles, potentially bolstering Kyiv's defences against similar Russian strikes.

title: Delhi Man, 20, Stabbed To Death By Mob, 2 Cops Who Tried To Save Him Injured
content: A team from the local police station found the victim, Irfan, in an injured state in B block of the locality.

title: 4 Killed In Massive Landslide In Nagaland, More Feared Dead
content: "Eight people are feared dead. We have been able to retrieve four bodies," Nagaland's Mon district deputy commissioner Wennyei Konyak told NDTV

title: Goa BJP MLA And Ex-State Minister Jennifer Monseratte Dies At 56
content: Chief Minister Pramod Sawant expressed grief at the demise of his party colleague, who represented the Taleigao assembly constituency in North Goa district.

title: Sena Corporator Who Assaulted Doctors Surrenders Day After Court Cancels Bail
content: A division bench of Acting Chief Justice Ravindra Ghuge and Justice Gautam Ankhad had overturned the Kalyan magistrate court's bail to Mhatre, citing criminal cases against him.

title: Air India Group To Restore Full Middle East Network This Winter: Nipun Aggarwal
content: He said the airline's biggest challenge during the recent tensions in the Middle East was not a fall in passenger demand but operational restrictions caused by airspace closures.

title: 7 Million Abusive Comments Aimed At Players, Staff Detected During World Cup
content: Earlier this month, FIFA said racial abuse accounted for 11% of detected offensive messages during the World Cup so far.

title: Study Reveals J&K Mountains Are Warming Up Faster Than Plains. Here's Why
content: The researchers analysed observations from 10 weather stations across Jammu and Kashmir and found that the region's climate is changing unevenly.

title: New Rule In Israel Allows Use Of Crocodiles To Stop Prison Escapes
content: Far-right National Security Minister Itamar Ben Gvir -- who reportedly proposed in December surrounding a prison for Palestinian detainees with crocodiles, a la Florida's "Alligator Alcatraz" immigration detention facility -- welcomed the decision.

title: Greater Noida vs New Gurugram: Which NCR Hotspot Delivers More Value
content: Both promise modern infrastructure. Both are witnessing massive public investment. Both are becoming magnets for premium housing.

title: Delhi Woman Lay In Pool Of Blood, Killer Husband Kept Changing Cities In UP
content: The 45-year-old woman was found in a pool of blood at her house in Delhi's Seelampur on Thursday evening.

content: The TOI Business Desk is a vigilant and dedicated team of journalists committed to delivering the latest and most relevant business news from around the world to readers of The Times of India. title: 'Should've scored 707, got 217': NTA finds OMR tampering after NEET aspirants flag score gaps
content: the controversy began when a student from Pune alleged he had received two different OMR sheets . the national testing agency concluded that the sheets in question were manipulated . students raised allegations of discrepancies in their entrance exam results .
